prevalence.value: common seasonal illness; highest in children 6 months to 3 years

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; can cause emergency visits

vulnerablePopulations: children with limited access to emergency care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis: barking cough, hoarseness, inspiratory stridor; severity assessment; exclude epiglottitis and foreign body

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with corticosteroids; severe cases need nebulized epinephrine and observation

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in APS/ER; imaging rarely needed
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Dexamethasone for most cases; nebulized epinephrine for moderate-severe stridor; supportive care
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Emergency service surges during viral seasons
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Effective low-cost treatments and strong pediatric guidelines
